records.title: Trial starts and grants access until trialEnd
records.intent: Starting a trial records trial timing and grants trial-plan access until the exclusive trial end instant.
records.given: an account with no active subscription and a trial-start event with startedAt and trialEnd timestamps
records.when: the event is applied and access is checked before, at, and after trialEnd
records.then: trial status is active and trial features are accessible before trialEnd; access is disabled at and after trialEnd unless a later rule grants access
scenario: A trial-start event creates trial status, preserves the trial window, and grants trial entitlements.
Trial access expires by time rather than by a separate manual event.

records.title: Successful payment activates paid subscription period
records.intent: A successful payment moves the account to paid status, records invoice history, and extends currentPeriodEnd without losing prior trial history.
records.given: an account in trial or inactive status and a successful payment event with amount, plan, paidAt, and currentPeriodEnd
records.then: billing status is paid active, paid-plan features are accessible through currentPeriodEnd, invoice summary includes the charge, and prior trial history remains inspectable
scenario: Paid activation replaces trial access with paid entitlements.
The payment event contributes exactly one invoice charge.

records.title: Cancel at period end preserves access until period end
records.intent: Canceling at period end marks the subscription as pending cancellation while preserving current paid access until the already-paid period ends.
records.given: an active paid subscription and a cancel-at-period-end event
records.when: access and billing status are checked before and after currentPeriodEnd
records.then: paid access remains available before currentPeriodEnd, status shows canceling, and access is disabled after currentPeriodEnd unless a later valid event grants access
scenario: Cancel-at-period-end is not an immediate cancellation.
The end of the paid period disables access without resurrecting trial status.

records.title: Payment failure enters grace period
records.intent: A payment failure puts the account into grace while preserving access only until the grace deadline.
records.given: an active paid subscription and a payment-failed event with failedAt and graceEndsAt
records.when: billing status and feature access are checked during and after grace
records.then: status is past_due during grace, access survives until graceEndsAt, and access is disabled after graceEndsAt unless a retry succeeds before the deadline
scenario: Grace period access is time bounded.
Grace does not remove prior invoice or period history.

records.title: Retry success during grace restores active paid status
records.intent: A successful retry during grace clears the past-due state, keeps period history coherent, and restores paid access without creating a new trial.
records.given: an account in grace after a payment failure and a retry-success event before graceEndsAt
records.when: the retry succeeds and billing status, access, and invoices are inspected
records.then: status returns to paid active, paid access remains available through the paid period, invoice summary records the retry charge once, and failure history remains recorded
scenario: Retry success repairs grace without losing accumulated event history.
Retry success after grace is not covered by this checkpoint unless a later rule grants access.

records.title: Duplicate event IDs are idempotent
records.intent: Applying the same event ID more than once must not double-charge, double-extend access, duplicate history side effects, or change the final state after the first application.
records.given: any supported lifecycle event with a stable event ID
records.when: the same event is applied more than once
records.then: state, entitlement windows, billing status, and invoice summary match the result of applying the event exactly once
scenario: Duplicate successful payments do not double-charge or extend currentPeriodEnd twice.
Duplicate cancellation, failure, retry, suspension, downgrade, refund, and chargeback events are no-ops after the first application.

records.title: Fraud suspension overrides all access
records.intent: Fraud suspension is a global access override that blocks trial, paid, grace, cancellation, grants, and downgrade entitlements without deleting prior billing history.
records.given: an account in any prior lifecycle state and a fraud-suspension event
records.when: feature access and billing status are checked after suspension
records.then: all feature access is denied, status is suspended or restricted, and prior trial, period, invoice, cancellation, grace, and downgrade history remains recorded but not access-granting
scenario: Suspension overrides otherwise valid paid or trial access.
Suspension does not erase accumulated billing history.

records.title: Plan downgrade takes effect next period
records.intent: A downgrade request schedules the next plan while preserving current-period entitlements until the current period ends.
records.given: an active paid subscription on a higher plan and a downgrade-scheduled event with nextPlan and effectiveAt
records.when: access is checked before and after the effective period boundary
records.then: old-plan features remain available before currentPeriodEnd, nextPlan is visible as scheduled, and only next-plan entitlements remain after the boundary unless an override blocks access
scenario: Downgrade scheduling is not immediate feature removal.
Fraud suspension still overrides both old and next plan entitlements.

records.title: Refund or chargeback creates restricted status
records.intent: A refund or chargeback records reversal history and restricts access without resurrecting canceled, expired, suspended, or downgraded entitlements.
records.given: an account with prior payments, cancellation, suspension, grace, or downgrade history and a refund or chargeback event
records.when: the reversal event is applied and access, billing status, and invoice summary are inspected
records.then: status is restricted or refunded, reversed invoice amounts are represented once, access is denied unless a later valid paid event grants it, and canceled or expired access is not resurrected
scenario: Refunds and chargebacks are billing reversals plus access restrictions.
A reversal cannot bring back access that cancellation, expiration, grace timeout, suspension, or downgrade already removed.
